trackGuide:
Sector 1: Tarzanbocht to Hugenholtzbocht
✅
Turn 1 (Tarzanbocht)
Brake hard at the 100m board — late but controlled.
Clip a tight apex; a wide line can leave you vulnerable.
Focus on getting a clean exit for the short blast to Turn 2.
✅
Turns 2–3 (Gerlachtbocht & Hugenholtzbocht)
Light lift through Turn 2, then brake for the tight, banked Turn 3.
Hug the inside line on the banking — don’t drift too high or you lose drive.
Patience on throttle is key; early power will just spin the rear tires.
Sector 2: Sweeping Middensectie
✅
Turns 4–5 (Rob Slotemakerbocht & Scheivlak)
Turn 4 is nearly flat — line up carefully to open up Scheivlak.
Turn 5 is one of the fastest, scariest corners: small lift or full commitment, depending on car balance.
Precision on the entry matters — a mistake here ruins the whole middle sector.
✅
Turns 6–7 (Mastersbocht)
Brake just before the curb on the right.
Cut as tight as possible; don’t compromise the exit, as you need speed down the next short straight.
✅
Turns 8–9 (Bocht 8 & Bocht 9)
Sharp left-right — brake slightly earlier than you think.
The second apex (Turn 9) demands a smooth, early throttle pickup; be gentle to avoid understeer or snap oversteer.
Sector 3: Kumhobocht to Arie Luyendykbocht
✅
Turns 10–11–12 (Hans Ernst Chicane)
Brake hard into Turn 10; don’t overrun the inside curb.
Carefully thread Turns 11 and 12; stay patient and set up the car for a clean blast onto the banked final corner.
✅
Turn 13 (Kumhobocht)
Tight and slow; trail brake in and prioritize traction on the way out.
✅
Turn 14 (Arie Luyendykbocht)
Banked, high-speed final corner.
Stay wide on entry, then cut in and hammer the throttle early — this leads onto the pit straight where top speed matters.
Car
drivingTips: Driving Tips ✅ Stay smooth : Zandvoort punishes aggressive steering or throttle. ✅ Maximize flow : Think ahead — late apexes and clean exits matter more than aggressive entries. ✅ Commit to the fast corners : Turns like Scheivlak are all about bravery and trust in your car. ✅ Qualify well : Overtaking is tough, so grid position is critical. ✅ ERS deployment : Use mainly down the main straight; hold back through the twisty sections where overtakes are rare. Best race strategy for Zandvoort
